message:
fix(fcp-crypto/cose): auto-synthesize canonical GRANTS on sign (br-8n0rm.8)

Every signed token the builder emits now carries the canonical
`fcp2_claims::GRANTS` shape in addition to whatever legacy fields
the caller set. Synthesis derives the GRANTS array from
`capability_id + operations` right before signing — no caller
changes required.

Why: 8n0rm.6 wants to delete the verifier's GRANTS→OPERATIONS
legacy fallback branch at capability.rs:1597-1621. That branch is
only safe to remove when every token on the wire already has the
canonical GRANTS. Rather than forcing a 50+ call-site migration of
`.operations(&[...])` → `.custom(GRANTS, ...)` across fcp-host +
connectors, this patch centralizes the canonicalization in one
place: `CapabilityTokenBuilder::sign`.

Semantics:
- If GRANTS is already set (via `.custom(GRANTS, ...)` or
  `.with_claims`), the synthesizer does nothing — respects the
  caller's intent.
- If GRANTS is absent and both capability_id + operations are
  present, synthesize an array of
  `{capability: cap_id, operation: op_n}` CBOR maps — matching the
  serde shape of `fcp_core::capability::CapabilityGrant` so the
  canonical verifier branch (capability.rs:1579) parses it
  natively.
- If GRANTS is absent AND either capability_id or operations is
  missing, synthesis is a no-op — the verifier will surface a
  MissingField error downstream with a clear diagnostic.

Implementation note: fcp-crypto has no dependency on fcp-core, so
the synthesizer builds the raw CBOR shape directly rather than
using `CapabilityGrant::serde`. This preserves the
fcp-auth-schema → fcp-crypto → fcp-core layering.

Tests:
- sign_synthesizes_grants_from_legacy_operations — verifies the
  synthesized CBOR matches CapabilityGrant serde output byte-
  equivalent (capability + operation text fields).
- sign_preserves_existing_grants_claim — pre-set GRANTS via
  .custom(...) survives untouched; does NOT get overwritten by the
  legacy capability_id + operations that may also be present.
- 259 existing fcp-core capability tests still green — synthesis is
  transparent.

Unblocks 8n0rm.6 removal of the legacy verifier fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
